title: Purely Passionate Mexican Pork Tacos Al Pastor
description: A fermented, tender, and romantic Mexican dish featuring raspberries and tempering chocolate, made with macrobiotic friendly ingredients, served in the form of tacos al pastor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine raspberries, dark chocolate, poblano peppers, onion, garlic, cumin, and coriander to make the marinade.
Place the pork shoulder in a large bowl and pour the marinade over it, making sure the pork is fully coated.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the pork to ferment.
Remove the pork from the marinade and place it in the oven to cook for 6 hours, or until the pork is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
While the pork is cooking, prepare the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Once the pork is done, shred it with two forks and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Assemble the tacos by placing a portion of the shredded pork onto a tortilla and serving immediately.
